name: Friends Of The North Country
description_short: HUD-certified nonprofit providing housing counseling, home repair assistance, and foreclosure intervention to North Country residents since 1981.
description_long:
Friends of the North Country, Inc. is a New York State Rural Preservation Corporation established in 1981 that has served the North Country region (Clinton, Franklin, and Essex Counties) for over 40 years. The organization is a HUD-certified, grant-funded nonprofit dedicated to addressing affordable housing challenges in upstate New York. Founded as a community development agency, Friends has grown into a multifaceted organization that combines direct client services with regional economic development initiatives.

Friends offers housing counseling through two trained certified counselors, foreclosure intervention services, home repair and replacement assistance, and adaptive living equipment for elderly and disabled residents. They help families address critical housing issues including furnace repair, faulty wiring, water contamination, roof leaks, structural problems, plumbing deterioration, and insulation deficiencies. The organization also provides ramps and adaptive equipment for elderly, disabled, and frail residents. Additionally, Friends engages in community planning, downtown redevelopment coordination, historic preservation designation and restoration, and infrastructure project management.

Friends distinguishes itself through its deep local roots, long operational history, and comprehensive approach combining direct housing assistance with broader community revitalization. The organization has successfully generated millions in Federal, State, and private investment in regional projects. They actively pursue grant funding to support their services and maintain trained housing counselors on staff. The organization serves local governments, nonprofits, and the general community across their service region.

As a nonprofit dependent on grant funding and donations, Friends cannot assist all residents requesting help despite their extensive efforts. Their services are geographically limited to the North Country region of upstate New York. While they offer valuable free or low-cost housing counseling and repair assistance, availability may be limited based on current grant funding and project capacity. Prospective clients should contact them directly regarding eligibility and current service availability.
pros: HUD-certified nonprofit with two trained housing counselors on staff providing professional guidance
40+ years of established operational history specifically serving North Country residents
Grant-funded services mean free or low-cost assistance rather than commercial lending terms
Comprehensive repair assistance covering furnaces, wiring, water systems, roofs, plumbing, and structural issues
Specialized services for vulnerable populations including elderly, disabled, and frail residents
Generated millions in Federal, State, and private investment demonstrating track record and expertise
Wheelchair-accessible office with telecommunications relay service for disabled access
cons: Services limited to Clinton, Franklin, and Essex Counties in upstate New York; not statewide or national
Cannot help all applicants due to limited grant funding and reliance on donations
Website lacks details on specific eligibility criteria, application timelines, or service wait times
No information provided on whether services are truly free or if there are income/asset limits
Limited transparency on average project scope, repair coverage amounts, or maximum assistance per household
best_for: Low-income homeowners in Clinton, Franklin, or Essex Counties facing critical home repair needs
Elderly or disabled North Country residents needing accessibility modifications and adaptive equipment
Families facing foreclosure seeking professional housing counseling from certified counselors
Homeowners seeking free guidance on affordable housing options and sustainable housing stability
diagnosis: Friends of the North Country is best for low-income homeowners in the upstate New York North Country region who need free or grant-funded housing counseling, home repair assistance, or foreclosure prevention from an established HUD-certified nonprofit. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to Clinton, Franklin, and Essex Counties and funding constraints that prevent them from serving all applicants despite their commitment and expertise.
services: Housing counseling from certified HUD-trained counselors
Home repair assistance (furnaces, wiring, roofing, plumbing, structural)
Ramps and adaptive living equipment for elderly and disabled residents
Home replacement assistance for severely deteriorated properties
Grant funding identification and application support
Federal and state grant project management
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
